operating_state: Revenue increased 45% to $12.7 billion driven by Mounjaro and Zepbound volume growth, with U.S. revenue up 49% and international up 38%. Mounjaro revenue reached $3.8 billion (113% increase) while Zepbound generated $2.3 billion versus $517 million prior year. Volume increases were partially offset by lower realized prices across incretin medicines and unfavorable foreign exchange impact of 2%.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved to 82.5% from 80.9% driven by improved production costs and favorable product mix, offsetting lower realized prices. Marketing, selling, and administrative expenses increased 26% supporting ongoing and future launches. Research and development expenses rose 8% reflecting continued pipeline investments.
material_events: Acquired Scorpion's PI3Kα inhibitor program STX-478 for $1.76 billion with associated acquired IPR&D charges of $1.57 billion. Issued $6.5 billion in fixed-rate notes in February to fund the acquisition and general business purposes. Repurchased $1.2 billion in shares under the $15 billion authorization program. Paid $1.35 billion in dividends at $1.50 per share.
macro_exposures: Inflation Reduction Act pricing pressures intensified with Jardiance selected for government price-setting effective 2026, with additional significant products expected in future selections. Potential tariff impacts on supply chain costs and operations, though pharmaceuticals currently exempt from certain tariffs. Foreign exchange headwinds primarily from euro, yen, and yuan fluctuations against the dollar.
forward_outlook: Manufacturing capacity expansion ongoing with additional capacity expected operational over next several years. Capital expenditures will increase meaningfully to support facility investments. Supply agreements for medicines in development could require up to $8 billion in payments if specified purchase amounts not met over agreement durations up to 8 years.
uncertainty_profile: Tirzepatide supply dynamics remain complex with periodic unavailability of certain presentations despite overall supply meeting demand. Counterfeit and compounded incretin production continues despite FDA confirmation of shortage end. EMA Committee issued negative opinion on donanemab for Alzheimer's treatment with company seeking re-examination. Ongoing litigation related to 340B program, insulin access, and pricing matters.
